desc:
Dense groves of sprawling trees populate this soggy valley. Some trees lie
upended and rotting in the muddy terrain. Poison ivy grows along the bark of the
trees, covering some of them from root to crown. The ground slopes down to a
dirty rivulet in the centre of this valley, fed by the rainfall draining down
the surrounding mountainsides.
name: Musty Copse

desc:
This cavern begins at a round hole at the top of a slope to the south. From
there, the cavern quickly expands, widening to dozens of yards between the
eastern and the western walls. The floor slants downwards to the north, while
the roof stays at the same height which causes the cavern to expand in height as
well. The walls, roof, and floor are of rough and uneven grey rock.
name: Wide Cavern

desc:
The cave is ovoid with considerably smooth, rock walls of mostly a dim grey
colour, interspersed with ash grey. In a few separate locations, thin, dark
roots protrude from the ceiling, clear evidence that trees or bushes with deep
roots grow above this cave.
name: Large Cavern

desc:
Every here and there, dim grey walls are interspersed with a greener hue,
ranging from subtle hints a few feet high to dark green stains near the bottom
of the cave. The walls end upwards into an inky darkness, hiding the ceiling
from sight. A few jagged and uneven edges break up otherwise smooth wall. Dark
brown streaks resembling a deep brown mud seemingly connect each edge to each
other. Some of the more jagged edges have broken off, creating a haphazard line
of pebble-sized rocks.
name: Vast Cavern

desc:
Smooth, grey rock walls line the cave, only to be interrupted by a crack in the
northern wall. The crack twists and turns in a semi-straight line from top to
bottom, as if splitting the cave in half, effectively becoming a focal point of
the large cave. A few small strands of discarded light brown moss lie scattered
on the worn cavern floor.
name: Immense Cavern

desc:
Featuring mostly even rock walls, very small rounded, irregular cones form a
distinguishing layer to both the top and the bottom of the cave. The glacous
stalactites grow longer and denser, their pointed edges reaching over two feet
long towards the middle of the cave. Its companions that litter the cave floor
are much more subdued, stalagmites adding an uneven surface in sharp contrast to
the grey walls. A thin layer of dirty water forms tiny puddles of black water on
the cavern's floor.
name: Vast Cavern

desc:
A spacious den has been excavated beneath a hill. The walls look stable and
solid, and are covered with scratch marks. Piles of leaves mixed with animal
hair lie at the back of the den. Some dry, gnawed-on bones litter the floor.
There is no meat on them and they do not seem part of a recent meal.
name: Den under the Hills

desc:
Claw marks cover the walls of this den. Thin roots poke through the roof of the
den, indicating that there is only a few feet of soil between the top of the
cave and a forest floor. A pile of leaves and twigs lies at the back of the den,
mixed with a lot of animal hair.
name: Den under the Hills

desc:
This junction of caverns opens into a small cave to the east, a large cavern to
the west, and to the north lies yet another dark opening. The cavern's moist
air, and widely spread moss covering the stones make the ground feel very
slippery. An occasional tiny breeze of fresh air seems to flush in from the
north.
name: Junction of Caverns

desc:
Grey walls of rough rock form the eastern and the western walls of this cavern.
As if uncertain if it's to be a great, vast cavern, or a narrow tunnel, in some
places the walls are no more than a dozen feets apart, while elsewhere they
separate by dozens of yards. There are bends and twists, blind corners and
shallow alcoves.
name: Dark, Winding Cavern

desc:
This niche is but a dark secluded corner of a network of caverns expanding out
of sight to the west. The roof is surprisingly high for such a small cave and
the upper reaches are hidden in shadows. The floor is strew with boulders
discoloured in shades of yellow, brown, and green by cave-living lichens.
name: Dark Cave

desc:
This cavern has been formed from boulders, rocks and debris fallen down from the
surrounding hillsides. Over the ages smaller rocks have covered the holes and
gaps to form a roof. The ground is littered with smaller stones and rocks and
nothing grows in this cave.
name: Natural Cavern

desc:
Rough grey rock form the walls, floor, and ceiling of this natural cavern. The
floor is worn in places, as if being trod upon countless times. A jagged opening
to the north is partly covered by bushes. A slope at the cavern's southern end,
winding this way and that in a zig-zag manner, allows for an ascent deeper into
the cave.
name: Shadowy Grotto

desc:
A forest of oak, fir and birch covers the wide floor of a valley. Hills rise to
the south, and the forest thins out there as if it was hesitant to approach
them. Bushes and shrubs take over as the forest recedes in the shadows of the
hills. A trampled animal trail can be espied worn into the crab grass in-between
bushes where a series of switchbacks allows passage into the rougher terrain.
name: Forested Valley Floor

desc:
Large and sprawling oaks cover the landscape here, their low-hanging branches
gnarled and scraping against one another. A thick layer of lichen has formed on
the treetops, creating a dense canopy that hardly allows any light to pass
through. The forest floor reflects this darkness, with dark musty fungi growing
amongst the matte black twisting roots.
name: Tangled Woodlands

desc:
Black and gnarled trees stand close together here, forming a dense forest.
Thorny shrubs grow between trees and anywhere there is the slightest opportunity
to thrive. Low hanging branches scrape the forest floor and thick, grey lichen
hang from them like veils. Black, twisting vines crawl up trunks to disappear in
the impenetrable canopy.
name: Dark Forest

desc:
The age old trees that grow here seem to bend under the weight of their own
boughs. Vine and lichen-draped branches droop, almost to the point of touching
the ground. The undergrowth has been flattened and trampled in the western part
of the forest, at the feet of steep cliffs that rise suddenly and mark the end
of the valley.
name: Lumbering Oaks

desc:
Warm, humid air fills this cavern. A vast and endless web of fungi and vines
covers the walls from floor to ceiling. A glowing mat of moss completely covers
the floor, leaving no sign of the rock beneath it. The floor slopes steeply
downward to the south, and numerous shrubs are visible outside the cavern
entrance to the east.
name: Fungal Cavern

desc:
Thorny bushes scratch against the gnarled and twisted trees here, growing up
against the bark and intertwining with the dark vines hanging down from the
trees' branches. The terrain becomes increasingly steep to the west; a foul
rivulet forms a cut down the slope and across the forest floor, running between
stained, mottled roots.
name: Overgrown Slope

desc:
Rows of tall oaks encircle a sloping vale filled with sprawling bushes. The
branches of the trees intertwine above and a canopy of broad leaves forms a
dense roof. The ground is covered in black moss and thick, knotted roots beneath
the trees, while the bushes in the deeper vale grow too dense to allow even a
glimpse of the soil beneath.
name: Twisted Canopy

desc:
The forest grows sparse in this north-western corner of a valley nestled among
the foothills of the Emyn-nu-Fuin. However, while the trees are few here,
brambles and thorn bushes grow in abundance. Dense dark green crab grass also
thrives in this region, covering the landscape in emerald swaths.
name: Brushwood Valley

desc:
Numerous stalactites hang down from the ceiling of this sloping cavern. The
walls of the cave are covered with grey fungus and water constantly drips down
from the ceiling. The slope slants down to the south where it narrows. A worn
path along the easiest route of ascent clearly shows that this passage is used
frequently.
name: Cavernous Slope

desc:
A vast cavern opens up to the north, the east and the west; to the south, it
tapers into a narrow passage. Great fissures scar the ceiling and water slowly
drops down from above. Mounds of bat guano lie on the cavern's floor, filling
the air with an acrid, pungent stench.
name: Fissured Cavern

desc:
A small, semicircular grotto opens up into a bigger cavern to the west. Great
slabs of rock slanted at various angles make up the walls of the grotto; these
slabs are covered with scattered patches of fungi. Several large cracks scar the
ceiling and water slowly drops down from above.
name: Fissured Cavern

desc:
Several shallow recesses, each less than ten feet deep, line the walls of this
dark cavern. Some of these openings seem to be covered with cobwebs, and round
cocoons hang from the walls. The floor is rough, with many sharp, protruding
rocks which make it difficult to walk upon. The ceiling is quite low compared to
that of the caverns stretching to the east, yet would still be out of reach for
even the tallest of trolls.
name: Fissured Cavern

desc:
Many stalactites hang from the ceiling in this large and murky cavern. A soft,
fresh breeze carries from high above where great fissures scar the ceiling
between the stalactites. The cavern walls are steep and made of brown and grey
stone; a passage opens up to the south. There are several wet patches on the
floor where slowly dripping water is soaked up by the soil.
name: Fissured Cavern

desc:
This dark tunnel comes to an abrupt end as a massive gate made of wood stands to
the north. An opening of some kind seems to lead to the east and another
downwards inside the mountain. Walls of this tunnel are rough and hard with some
marks from the tools used to gnaw into the stone.
name: Massive Wooden Gate

desc:
The walls of this room look somewhat more polished than those of the passage
immediately to the west. Wooden planks cover the floor, and there is a fireplace
in the corner which appears to be used for cooking. A crude bed made of hay and
fur sits on the floor next to the eastern wall. This room offers a clear view of
a heavy wooden gate to the west.
name: Guard Room

desc:
A dark tunnel descending from above comes to a sudden end at a massive wooden
gate bolted securely into the rock walls from both sides. This crude creation
appears quite bulky and strong despite the lack of finesse in its construction.
The tunnel narrows as it approaches the gate and when it reaches it, the tunnel
is only as high and as wide as the gate is.
name: Dark Tunnel at a Gate

desc:
A small and narrow tunnel has been delved into the mountain, falling steeply as
it descends to the south. Tool marks are visible on the walls, and the ground
has been worn smooth by heavy traffic. The ceiling has been shored up with
numerous wooden beams.
name: Dark Tunnel
